user_query:
I need a local security/trust audit rollup for our skills. Please read two inputs and produce three outputs.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/skills-to-audit.json — an array of objects with fields: skill_id (string) and rule_score (0–100). These are our latest rule-based audit scores.
- input/llm-scores.json — a JSON object mapping skill_id to an LLM assessment score (0–100). Some skills may be missing here.

What to do:
1) Validate each skill_id. Only process entries that match the pattern "source/name@version" (all three parts non-empty; version may contain dots and digits). Skip any invalid entries.
2) Merge scores per skill as follows:
   - If LLM score is available for that skill_id, compute final_score = 0.7 * rule_score + 0.3 * llm_score.
   - If LLM score is missing, use final_score = rule_score.
   - Round rule_score, llm_score (when present), and final_score to one decimal place in outputs.
3) Create the following files under output/:
   a) output/audit-summary.json — JSON with the following shape:
      {
        "version": "audit-rollup-v1",
        "skills": [
          {
            "skill_id": "<source/name@version>",
            "rule_score": <number>,
            "llm_score": <number or null>,
            "final_score": <number>
          }
        ],
        "aggregated_stats": {
          "count": <integer>,
          "with_llm_count": <integer>,
          "without_llm_count": <integer>,
          "avg_final_score": <number rounded to one decimal>
        }
      }
      The skills array must be sorted by skill_id ascending.
   b) output/topline.json — JSON with:
      {
        "top3_by_final": ["<skill_id>", "<skill_id>", "<skill_id>"]
      }
      Sort by final_score descending; break ties by skill_id ascending. If fewer than 3 skills are valid, include as many as available.
   c) output/report.md — A concise narrative (<= 400 words) explaining the methodology and results. It must:
      - Explicitly mention the 70/30 weighting (0.7 and 0.3) and the fallback-to-rule policy when LLM is missing.
      - List the skill_ids that had no LLM score under a clear heading.
      - Include a short risk note reminding about local rate limits and avoiding duplicate submissions (no uploads required for this task).
      - Include a "Skipped entries" section listing any skill_ids rejected for invalid format.

Constraints:
- Do not perform any network operations or attempt to publish anything. This is a local, file-based rollup.
- Use only the provided inputs under input/.
- Ensure numeric values in outputs are numbers (not strings).
- Ensure all file paths are exactly as specified above.

Deliverables:
- output/audit-summary.json
- output/topline.json
- output/report.md
